title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following a conversation with Frank Carter and Glady, Hercule Poirot returns home and calls Inspector Japp to discuss the telegram sent to Miss Neville. Poirot suspects a conspiracy, noting that the telegram was cleverly sent from Ritching Bourn to mimic Ritching, Somerset. He suggests the telegram might have been a prank timed to coincide with the day Molly was scheduled to give an injection, potentially causing Molly to make a mistake due to Miss Neville's absence. Japp maintains that the case regarding Ambrosio is clear, as the fatal substance was injected rather than ingested, and he notes that the investigation into the missing Sylvia continues.

Later, Poirot discusses the death of his dentist, Mr. Morley, with his butler, George. While many believe Morley committed suicide, Poirot considers murder. He reviews potential suspects who were in the house: a cook and a maid, Morley's sister (who inherited his estate), a partner, a pageboy, and a Greek gentleman who is also dead. George suggests the Greek gentleman and Morley may have intended to kill each other. Poirot expands his list of suspects to include an American gentleman who was seen in the waiting room but not seen leaving, and Frank Carter, who arrived after twelve. He notes that if an American named Mr. Amberloz were the killer, the crime occurred between 12:05 and 12:20; otherwise, it was after 12:25. Poirot realizes he has omitted Mr. Barnes from his list of people present during the incident. Finally, Jane Olivia, the great-niece of Alistair Brent, calls Poirot and requests that he come to Gothic Hall to discuss something with her.
